question.text: Under PMLA, the term "scheduled offence" means offences specified under:

answer.text: Only Part A of the Schedule
Part A and Part B of the Schedule only
Part A, Part B (if total value is Rs. 1 crore or more), and Part C of the Schedule
Part A (if total value is Rs. 1 crore or more) and Part C of the Schedule

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Section 2(y) defines "scheduled offence" to include: (i) offences under Part A — no monetary threshold, (ii) offences under Part B — only if the total value involved is Rs. 1 crore or more, and (iii) offences under Part C — cross-border implications, no monetary threshold.
**The Trap:** The monetary threshold of Rs. 1 crore applies ONLY to Part B offences, not Part A or Part C. Many students incorrectly assume the threshold applies to all parts. Part A and Part C offences are scheduled offences regardless of value.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Understanding the three parts (A, B, C) and their thresholds is critical for Chapter 4 (Scheduled Offences) questions in NISM XXIV. This definition from Section 2 sets the foundation.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Part B mainly covers Customs Act offences (Section 132 — false declaration, false documents). If the value involved is less than Rs. 1 crore, it is NOT a scheduled offence under Part B.
